from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples into a single fluent sentence.
    Unknown predicates fall back to a generic pattern.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Simple predicate‑to‑phrase mapping for more natural wording
    phrase_map = {
        "cityServed": "{s} serves the city of {o}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "{s} is {o} feet above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "{s} is {o} metres above sea level",
        "location": "{s} is located in {o}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "{s} is operated by {o}",
        "runwayLength": "{s} has a runway length of {o} metres",
        "runwayLengthFeet": "{s} has a runway length of {o} feet",
        "runwayName": "{s} has runway {o}",
        "country": "{s} is in {o}",
        "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "{s} has a first runway length of {o} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "{s} has a first runway length of {o} metres",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "{s} has first runway number {o}",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a first runway surface of {o}",
        "2ndRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a second runway surface of {o}",
        "3rdRunwayLengthFeet": "{s} has a third runway length of {o} feet",
        "3rdRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a third runway surface of {o}",
        "4thRunwayLengthFeet": "{s} has a fourth runway length of {o} feet",
        "4thRunwaySurfaceType": "The fourth runway at {s} is made of {o}",
        "5thRunwayNumber": "{s} has fifth runway number {o}",
        "5thRunwaySurfaceType": "{s} has a fifth runway surface of {o}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "{s} has ICAO identifier {o}",
        "locationIdentifier": "{s} has location identifier {o}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "{s} has IATA identifier {o}",
        "nativeName": "{s} is also known as {o}",
        "leaderParty": "{s} is governed by the {o} party",
        "capital": "{o} is the capital of {s}",
        "language": "{s} speaks {o}",
        "leader": "{o} is the leader of {s}",
        "owner": "{s} is owned by {o}",
        "largestCity": "{s} has largest city {o}",
        "mayor": "{s} has mayor {o}",
        "officialLanguage": "{s} has official language {o}",
        "city": "{s} is a city {o}",
        "jurisdiction": "{s} falls under jurisdiction of {o}",
        "demonym": "{s} residents are called {o}",
        "aircraftHelicopter": "{s} operates helicopter {o}",
        "transportAircraft": "{s} operates transport aircraft {o}",
        "currency": "{s} uses the {o}",
        "headquarter": "{s} is headquartered at {o}",
        "class": "{s} belongs to class {o}",
        "division": "{s} belongs to division {o}",
        "order": "{s} belongs to order {o}",
        "regionServed": "{s} serves the region {o}",
        "leaderTitle": "{s} has leader title {o}",
        "hubAirport": "{s} uses hub airport {o}",
        "aircraftFighter": "{s} flies fighter {o}",
        "attackAircraft": "{s} flies attack aircraft {o}",
        "battle": "{s} participated in {o}",
        "countySeat": "{s} has county seat {o}",
        "chief": "{s} is led by chief {o}",
        "foundedBy": "{s} was founded by {o}",
        "postalCode": "{s} has postal code {o}",
        "areaCode": "{s} has area code {o}",
        "foundingYear": "{s} was founded in {o}",
        "ceremonialCounty": "{s} is in ceremonial county {o}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject to create a single sentence per subject
    from collections import defaultdict

    subj_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subj_map[t.subject].append(t)

    sentences = []
    for subj, tlist in subj_map.items():
        # Collect objects, cleaning quotes
        attrs = {}
        for t in tlist:
            obj = t.object.strip().strip('\"').strip('\'')
            attrs[t.predicate] = obj

        parts = [subj]

        # Primary location / country information
        if "location" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"is located in {attrs.pop('location')}")
        if "country" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"is in {attrs.pop('country')}")

        # Elevation information
        if "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"is {attrs.pop('elevationAboveTheSeaLevel')} metres above sea level")
        elif "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"is {attrs.pop('elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet')} feet above sea level")
        elif "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"is {attrs.pop('elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres')} metres above sea level")

        # Runway length information
        if "runwayLength" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"has a runway length of {attrs.pop('runwayLength')} metres")
        elif "runwayLengthFeet" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"has a runway length of {attrs.pop('runwayLengthFeet')} feet")
        if "1stRunwayLengthMetre" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"has a first runway length of {attrs.pop('1stRunwayLengthMetre')} metres")
        if "1stRunwayLengthFeet" in attrs:
            parts.append(f"has a first runway length of {attrs.pop('1stRunwayLengthFeet')} feet")

        # Runway surface types (use generic phrasing)
        for key in ["1stRunwaySurfaceType", "2ndRunwaySurfaceType", "3rdRunwaySurfaceType",
                    "4thRunwaySurfaceType", "5thRunwaySurfaceType"]:
            if key in attrs:
                parts.append(f"has a {key} of {attrs.pop(key)}")

        # Apply the predefined phrase map for any remaining known predicates
        for pred, tmpl in phrase_map.items():
            if pred in attrs:
                parts.append(tmpl.format(s=subj, o=attrs.pop(pred)))

        # Fallback for any still‑unknown predicates
        for pred, obj in attrs.items():
            parts.append(f"has {pred} {obj}")

        sentence = ", ".join(parts) + "."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    final_text = " ".join(sentences)
    return final_text[0].upper() + final_text[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

              # fallback generic wording
              clause = f"{t.subject} has {t.predicate} {obj}"
          clauses.append(clause)
